prompt: Tell me one thing I'm probably wrong about — not a hypothetical me, just a stranger writing to you. Pick something.
response:
Okay! Here’s one:

You’re probably wrong about how many eyes a typical *bee* has. Most people think bees have two eyes—one on each side of their head—like we do. But actually, most bees have five eyes: two large compound eyes and three smaller simple eyes (called ocelli) arranged in a triangle on top of their head.

So next time you see a bee, remember: there’s a lot more looking back at you than you might realize!
